subject: Biology
title: Bottleneck and Founder Effects
description: Learn Bottleneck and Founder Effects in one minute.

hook.text: Northern elephant seals were hunted to just 20 individuals. Today's population of 200,000 is genetically almost identical.

buildup.text: A population bottleneck occurs when a disaster slashes numbers, randomly eliminating most genetic diversity.

discovery.text: The founder effect happens when a small group colonizes a new area, carrying only a fraction of the original gene pool.

twist.text: Rare genetic diseases are common in some communities because their founders happened to carry those alleles.

climax.text: Cheetahs are so genetically uniform from a past bottleneck that they can accept skin grafts from any other cheetah.

punchline.text: Survival doesn't guarantee diversity. Just luck.

quiz.question: Why are cheetahs genetically almost identical?
options: A past population bottleneck eliminated most diversity
They all live in the same area
